violations.description: Integration test file is 462 lines - too complex to maintain effectively
violations.suggestion: Split into separate files by feature area (e.g., user-api-capabilities.test.ts, user-api-validation.test.ts)
violations.code_snippet: describe('Story 1.1: User API Business Logic', () => { /* 462 lines */ });

violations.description: Integration test file is 455 lines - should be split for better maintainability
violations.suggestion: Split into separate files: pbac-authorization.test.ts, pbac-helper-functions.test.ts, pbac-route-mapping.test.ts

violations.description: Admin login code repeated in every test (lines 50-64, 100-106, 162-169, 213-220)
violations.suggestion: Extract to helper function: async function loginAsAdmin(page) { ... }

violations.description: User creation via admin pattern duplicated (lines 57-75, 126-143)
violations.suggestion: Extract to helper function: async function createUserViaAdmin(page, userData) { ... }

violations.description: Login flow repeated across all tests (clear fields, type credentials, click submit)
violations.suggestion: Extract to helper function or use Page Object Model pattern

violations.description: E2E test file is 234 lines - consider splitting by feature
E2E test file is 192 lines - consider splitting
E2E test file is 234 lines - consider splitting
violations.code_snippet: test.describe('Story 1.1: Forced Password Reset Flow', () => { /* 234 lines */ });

violations.description: Test has excessive console logging and debugging code (lines 118-129)
violations.suggestion: Remove debug logging or use conditional logging based on environment

violations.description: Console error listeners repeated in multiple tests
violations.suggestion: Move to fixture or test-wide setup, not in individual tests

recommendations: Split large integration test files (>400 lines) into smaller, focused files
Extract duplicate login code into helper functions or use Page Object Model pattern
Create shared test utilities for common operations (login, user creation, password change)
Remove or conditionally enable debug console.log statements in tests
Use test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' }) for tests that must run sequentially due to shared data
summary: Tests have good structure with test IDs and priority markers, but suffer from code duplication and large file sizes. 2 HIGH violations (files >400 lines), 6 MEDIUM violations (duplicate code and file size), 2 LOW violations (debug code).
